title: Microbenchmark with JMH and report honest numbers
description: Run a JMH benchmark with warmup, forks, and Blackhole to defeat JIT dead-code elimination, then report throughput with error bounds.
body: Use when validating a JVM performance change. Actions: write a @Benchmark method, consume results via Blackhole (or return them) so the JIT cannot eliminate the work, configure @Warmup and @Measurement iterations and @Fork(>=2) to escape one-VM bias, run with `java -jar benchmarks.jar`, and report the score, unit, and ± error. Do not benchmark in a plain main() (no warmup means you measure the interpreter/C1, not steady state). This skill exposes capability only and must not run on a noisy shared host without runtime permission. Validation signals include overlapping error bars indicating no real change, or a clear non-overlapping improvement, and stable numbers across forks. Risk boundary: a missing Blackhole lets the JIT delete the loop yielding impossibly fast times; flag near-zero or implausible results rather than reporting them as wins.
